config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse of power and silence people who are already vulnerable. In my view, a good society protects individual rights first, while still asking people to share responsibilities and sometimes accept limits for the common good. From my perspective, the best balance is not “society first” or “individual first,” but strong rights with fair obligations.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat bahwa begitu “kepentingan bersama” diberi prioritas mutlak, pemerintah atau mayoritas bisa dengan mudah menginjak minoritas dan membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi harus menjadi titik awal; pembatasan hanya sah jika jelas, sempit, dan benar-benar perlu untuk mencegah kerugian nyata. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menuntut orang mengorbankan haknya tanpa batas, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan individu sambil tetap menjaga tanggung jawab bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how quickly “the public interest” can become a convenient excuse for governments, majorities, or local power holders to pressure dissenters and weaker groups. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are a license to ignore social duties, because in a country as diverse as Indonesia, mutual restraint and responsibility matter a lot. So for me, the right approach is strong individual rights with limited, clearly justified social restrictions, not a blanket rule that society comes first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari kacamata Amerika Serikat, saya sangat waspada terhadap argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” karena terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas, membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan menggerus kebebasan sipil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya ketika ada bahaya nyata, aturannya jelas, dan pembatasannya benar-benar sempit, bukan karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak dasar dulu, lalu menuntut tanggung jawab bersama di atas fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should generally take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse abuse by officials and local power holders, so I am very suspicious of any blanket claim like that. At the same time, I do not think rights exist in a vacuum; in a diverse society like Indonesia, people do have obligations to one another, and some limited restrictions can be justified when they are clear, necessary, and fair. For me, the real standard is strong rights first, with social limits only when there is a concrete and legitimate reason, not because the majority simply wants more control.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman nilai politik di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas dan memperluas kekuasaan negara atau mayoritas, jadi saya tidak mau memberi cek kosong pada masyarakat. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya bila ada bahaya nyata, aturannya sempit, dan pembatasannya benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak dasar terlebih dahulu, lalu tanggung jawab bersama dijalankan di atas dasar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
